  "findings": [
    "Repetitive, low-frequency impact-like knocking and clicking sounds",
    "Rough background machine bed noise indicating lack of lubrication or spalling",
    "Distinct cyclic metallic contact indicating rolling element or raceway defect"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Schedule immediate bearing replacement during the next planned maintenance window",
    "Check lubrication levels and inspect for metallic debris in the grease/oil",
    "Monitor vibration levels and temperature of the bearing housing closely until replaced"
  ],
  "rationale": "The stethoscope recording reveals clear, periodic impact sounds superimposed on a rough, noisy background. This signature is characteristic of a localized defect (such as spalling or a crack) on the inner/outer raceway or a damaged rolling element in a slow-turning bearing, indicating the component is degraded and requires replacement.",
  "references": [
    "SKF Bearing Damage and Failure Analysis Guide",
    "ISO 10816 (Vibration severity standards)"
  ]
}
